controls.riskAddressed: Risk that emerging threats are not identified early enough to allow effective mitigation, or that high-value opportunities are overlooked due to lack of structured identification process
controls.testingApproach: Review of MTO board and tool entries, interview with Production Team, assessment of threat identification timeliness, verification of action plan closure rates

controls.subProcess: Impact Assessment Screening and Project Categorization
controls.controlName: Impact Assessment Screening and Project Risk Categorization
controls.controlStatement: Who: Impact Assessment SME with project management and environmental/social teams | What: Screen proposed projects for environmental, social, and health impacts; categorize project risk level (A/B/C) to determine assurance scope | When: During earliest project phases (identify/assess) before major design commitments | Where: IA Screening Note Tool | Why: Identify early whether comprehensive impact assessment is required and determine scope of environmental/social management effort needed | How: Desktop assessment of project characteristics against ESH sensitivity factors; identify baseline concerns; review against critical habitat/deforestation triggers; determine category; assess showstoppers | Evidence: Completed IA Screening Note Tool with documented project category, risk rationale, and identified next steps for IA process
controls.riskAddressed: Risk that projects with significant environmental or social impacts proceed without adequate assessment and mitigation planning, potentially causing unacceptable impacts or regulatory non-compliance
controls.testingApproach: Review of IA Screening Notes, interview with IA SME and project team, verification of category assignments against project characteristics, assessment of early risk identification

controls.subProcess: Impact Assessment Scoping and Baseline Definition
controls.controlName: Impact Assessment Scoping and Study Design
controls.controlStatement: Who: IA SME with project team, functional specialists, and external stakeholders | What: Define scope and methodology for impact assessment studies including baseline definition and impact study parameters | When: Following IA screening, during select/define project phases | Where: IA Scoping Report | Why: Focus impact assessment on most significant issues, define appropriate data collection, and establish clear baseline for impact evaluation | How: Identify impact sources and pathways; engage stakeholders for input; define significance criteria; scope baseline studies and required expert assessments; develop Terms of Reference for contractors | Evidence: Signed scoping report with defined impact areas, baseline study scope, significance criteria, stakeholder engagement plan, and study budget/schedule
controls.riskAddressed: Risk that impact assessment studies are not appropriately scoped, leading to missed significant impacts or inadequate baseline for evaluating project effects
controls.testingApproach: Review of IA Scoping Report, assessment of stakeholder engagement process, verification of baseline scope alignment with identified impacts

controls.controlName: Management of Change Process for System and Operational Changes
controls.controlStatement: Who: Asset/Operations leadership with support of technical authorities and process safety team | What: Assess, approve, and implement changes to facilities, equipment, procedures, or operations using structured MOC process | When: Before any change is implemented | Where: MOC system/database with documented assessment and approval records | Why: Ensure changes are evaluated for HSSE and operational risks and that all necessary modifications are completed before implementation | How: Complete MOC application with change description and rationale; assess design/operational/training impacts; identify required modifications; obtain multidisciplinary approvals; verify implementation; document formal MOC closure | Evidence: Completed MOC with supporting documentation (risk assessment, design packages, procedure updates, training records, implementation verification) retained per records requirements
controls.riskAddressed: Risk that changes are implemented without adequate assessment of consequences, leading to new hazards, barrier failures, or operational problems
controls.testingApproach: Review of MOC documentation, interview with operations leadership, assessment of risk assessment quality, verification of implementation completeness

controls.controlName: Process Safety Basic Requirements Compliance and Gap Closure
controls.controlStatement: Who: Asset/Business leadership with HSSE leadership and technical authorities | What: Ensure all Process Safety Basic Requirements are implemented and gaps are identified and closed | When: During annual AIPSM review, management reviews, and continuously through operational assurance | Where: AIPSM review documents, management system implementation records | Why: Provide baseline safeguards that all assets must maintain to manage major process safety hazards | How: Assess compliance against each PSBR element; document current status; identify gaps; develop corrective actions with timelines; implement; verify closure | Evidence: PSBR compliance assessment documenting status against each requirement, action plans for gaps, and closure documentation
controls.riskAddressed: Risk that fundamental process safety safeguards are not in place, leaving assets vulnerable to major hazard incidents
controls.testingApproach: Review of PSBR compliance assessment, interview with HSSE leadership, observation of implementations, verification of gap closure actions

controls.controlName: Hazards and Effects Management Process Implementation
controls.controlStatement: Who: HEMP Process Owner and Site Process Owner with support of multidisciplinary team | What: Execute Hazards and Effects Management Process to identify, analyze, and manage process safety hazards across asset lifecycle | When: During project design (DEM1), operating asset management, and management of change | Where: Documented in Hazards and Effects Register maintained in asset systems | Why: Provide systematic identification and assessment of major process safety hazards to support ALARP demonstration and barrier management | How: Conduct hazard identification (HAZID, HAZOP); assess consequences and likelihood; determine RAM rating; identify and evaluate barriers; apply mitigation hierarchy; document in HER | Evidence: Hazards and Effects Register with documented hazards, RAM ratings, barrier information, ALARP statements, and action status
controls.riskAddressed: Risk that major hazards are not identified or evaluated, leading to lack of appropriate controls for significant process safety risks
controls.testingApproach: Review of HEMP study documentation and HER, assessment of hazard identification completeness, interview with team members, verification of barrier adequacy

controls.controlName: Emergency Response Plan Development and Drill Execution
controls.controlStatement: Who: Operations leadership and emergency response coordinator with support of HSSE and community relations teams | What: Develop and maintain emergency response plans; conduct regular drills to verify plan effectiveness and personnel capability | When: Plans developed during project design; drills conducted at planned frequency (typically annually or per regulatory requirement) | Where: Emergency response procedures, site emergency plan, drill records | Why: Ensure rapid and effective response to emergency situations to protect personnel, public, and environment | How: Identify credible emergency scenarios; develop response procedures; assign roles and responsibilities; conduct awareness training; execute drills with observation and after-action review; update plans based on learning | Evidence: Emergency response plan, training records showing personnel participation, drill records with observation notes, and documented improvements from after-action reviews
controls.riskAddressed: Risk that emergency response is slow or ineffective due to inadequate planning or insufficient personnel training, leading to personnel injury or environmental impact
controls.testingApproach: Review of emergency response plans, interview with response team members, observation of drills, assessment of training records, evaluation of after-action review effectiveness
